question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a full EVM transaction replay via the opcodes endpoint without consuming any gas tokens from the gasLimitBucket, because throttleOpcodeRequest() only checks the opcodeRateLimitBucket and never deducts from the gas bucket, allowing unlimited gas-intensive replays that bypass the gas-per-second fee design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractDebugParameters always sets gasPrice=0 (no explicit initialization, field defaults to 0) to replay a historical transaction with zero gas price, causing the EVM to compute fees at a rate outside the original transaction's design parameters, effectively replaying high-value transactions at zero cost?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transactionIdOrHash referencing a historical transaction with a very high gasLimit, causing OpcodeServiceImpl.getGasLimit() to pass that large gas value into ContractDebugParameters without any upper-bound cap, thereby replaying the transaction with a gas limit that exceeds the design-intended per-request fee ceiling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the scaleGas() threshold in ThrottleProperties (gas <= 10,000 returns 0 tokens consumed) by replaying a transaction whose gasLimit is exactly at or below 10,000, causing the gas throttle bucket to consume zero tokens and allowing the replay to proceed as if no gas fee was incurred, violating the gas-per-second design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer overflow in OpcodeContext initialization (new OpcodeContext(opcodeRequest, (int) params.getGas() / 3)) where a gas value near Long.MAX_VALUE causes the cast to int to overflow and produce a negative ArrayList initial capacity, potentially causing an exception that bypasses normal fee accounting in the replay path?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the BlockType.LATEST fallback in OpcodeServiceImpl.buildCallServiceParameters() by referencing a transaction whose record file is missing, causing the replay to execute against the current state rather than the historical state, and thereby compute gas fees using a different (potentially cheaper) fee schedule than the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractDebugService.processOpcodeCall() sets the context timestamp to consensusTimestamp - 1 (one nanosecond before the transaction), causing the EVM replay to load a state where fee-related system files (e.g., exchange rate, fee schedule) differ from those at the actual transaction time, resulting in fee computation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call the opcodes endpoint at the maximum allowed rate (opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1) with transactions that have the maximum possible gasLimit, and since the gas bucket is never consumed for opcode requests, accumulate unbounded gas consumption that would be throttled on the regular /call endpoint, effectively modifying the effective fee rate outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TransactionIdParameter with an extremely large seconds value (up to 19 digits per the regex ^(\\d{1,19})-(\\d{1,9})$) that overflows convertToNanosMax(), causing the lookup to match an unintended transaction record and replay it with a different gas limit than expected, modifying effective fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getValue() method in OpcodeServiceImpl, which calls new BigInteger(ethereumTransaction.getValue()) on a raw byte array, by referencing a transaction where the stored value bytes represent a number larger than the original transaction's declared value, causing the replay to execute with an inflated value parameter that affects fee-related balance checks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual gas source in getGasLimit() (ethereumTransaction.getGasLimit() takes priority over contractResult.getGasLimit()) by referencing a transaction where the EthereumTransaction record has a higher gasLimit than the ContractResult record, causing the replay to use a higher gas limit than what was actually charged in the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the validateAcceptEncodingHeader() check by sending a crafted Accept-Encoding header value that contains 'gzip' as a substring of a larger token (e.g., 'x-gzip-custom'), satisfying the contains('gzip') check while potentially causing downstream decompression behavior that inflates response size and server-side processing cost outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restoreGasToBucket() is never called for opcode replay paths (ContractDebugService does not invoke the gas bucket restore logic that ContractCallService uses), meaning that even if gas were consumed, the refund mechanism designed to return unused gas to the bucket is skipped, breaking the gas fee accounting design?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the validatePayerBalance() short-circuit in ContractCallContext (returns false when gasPrice=0 and value=0) by relying on the fact that ContractDebugParameters always has gasPrice=0, causing the replay to skip payer balance validation entirely and execute transactions that would have been rejected due to insufficient balance in the original fee design?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent opcode requests (racing against the opcodeRateLimitBucket which uses a non-synchronized Bucket.builder().build() without SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to bypass the 1-request-per-second limit, allowing multiple simultaneous high-gas replays that collectively exceed the design-intended fee rate?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the memory=true and storage=true query parameters to force the EVM replay to capture full memory and storage snapshots at every opcode step, dramatically increasing server-side computation cost beyond what the single-token opcode rate limit was designed to account for, effectively consuming resources at a rate outside the fee design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user reference a CONTRACTCREATEINSTANCE transaction via transactionIdOrHash, causing getReceiverAddress() to return EMPTY_ADDRESS and the replay to execute a contract creation with the original transaction's gas limit but at gasPrice=0, bypassing the fee parameters that were applied during the original creation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionIdParameter regex allowing up to 9 nanosecond digits (\\d{1,9}) to supply a nanos value of 999999999, and combined with a boundary seconds value, cause convertToNanosMax() to produce a timestamp that maps to a different transaction than intended, replaying it with a mismatched gas limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that OpcodeServiceImpl.processOpcodeCall() calls ContractCallContext.run() which creates a fresh context, and within that context the gasRequirement field (settable via setGasRequirement()) is never bounded, allowing the EVM replay to set an arbitrary gas requirement that differs from the original transaction's fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply stack=true, memory=true, storage=true simultaneously to maximize per-opcode data capture, causing the OpcodeContext.addOpcodes() to accumulate a list of opcodes whose size is bounded only by (int) params.getGas() / 3, and for a transaction with gas=Long.MAX_VALUE this overflows to a negative capacity, causing an OutOfMemoryError or negative-capacity ArrayList exception that disrupts fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCallDataBytes() fallback logic (uses contractResult.getFunctionParameters() when ethereumTransaction is null) to replay a transaction where the stored function parameters differ from the original call data, causing the EVM to execute a different code path with different gas consumption than the original transaction's fee parameters anticipated?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getEthereumDataBytes() method returning the raw ethereum transaction data (ethereumTransaction.getData()) which may contain a different gas price field than what was recorded in contractResult, causing the EVM replay to interpret a different gas price than the original transaction used, modifying effective fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user reference a transaction hash that maps to a ContractTransactionHash record with a payerAccountId that differs from the actual transaction payer, causing getSenderAddress() to resolve a different sender address and bypass the payer balance validation that enforces fee design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionList.getFirst() call in the TransactionIdParameter path, where multiple transactions share the same payerAccountId and validStartNs, causing the replay to use the first transaction's gas limit (which may be higher than the intended one) and execute with fees outside the design parameters of the target transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractDebugParameters.isEstimate is always false (annotated @AssertFalse) to ensure the replay always goes through the full fee validation path, but since gasPrice=0, the fee validation in validatePayerBalance() is skipped, allowing the replay to proceed without the balance checks that enforce fee design parameters?
